changes_made: Renamed k8s_networkpolicy_allow_by_exception to k8s_networkpolicy_default_deny_with_exceptions for clarity and consistency with existing patterns.
Renamed k8s_admission_controller_software_whitelist to k8s_admission_controller_software_whitelist_enabled to clearly indicate a pass/fail condition.
Renamed k8s_pod_security_standard_least_functionality to k8s_pod_security_least_functionality_enforced for better alignment with naming conventions.
review_notes: The functions map well to Kubernetes security controls related to network policies, admission controllers, and pod security. However, naming improvements were necessary for clarity and consistency with established patterns. The changes ensure the function names are descriptive and clearly indicate the security checks being performed.
naming_improvements: Ensure function names clearly indicate the security check and its pass/fail condition.
Use consistent terminology such as 'default_deny' for network policies to align with common practices.
title: Least Functionality | Authorized Software — Allow-by-exception
step1_reasoning: The control CM-7 (5) focuses on ensuring that only authorized software is allowed, following an allow-by-exception model. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to network policies that restrict ingress and egress traffic by default, allowing only specified exceptions. Additionally, admission controllers can enforce software whitelisting by ensuring only approved images or configurations are deployed. Pod security standards can be used to ensure pods have the least functionality necessary, aligning with the control's intent.
